headline: Can CME Group Retain Its Beat Streak This Earnings Season?

headline: US FINANCIAL 15 SPLIT CORP. Preferred Dividend Declared
summary: TORONTO, April 20, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- US Financial 15 Split Corp ("US Financial 15") declares its monthly distribution of $0.06850 for each Preferred share, or 10.00% annually based on the previous month end net asset value. Distributions are payable May 8, 2026 to shareholders on record as at April 30, 2026. US Financial 15 invests in a portfolio primarily consisting of 15 U.S. financial services companies as follows: American Express, Bank of America, Bank of New York Mellon Corp., Citig

headline: CME Cross Margin Expansion Puts Capital Efficiency And Valuation In Focus
summary: CME Group (NasdaqGS:CME) and DTCC received regulatory approval to extend cross-margining to end-user clients. The program covers U.S. Treasury securities and interest rate futures transactions. Clients of dually registered broker/dealers and futures commission merchants can access these cross-margining benefits for the first time. The change is linked to new SEC clearing mandates that raise the importance of capital and margin efficiency. CME Group, which runs major futures and options...

headline: Is CME Group (CME) Pricing In Too Much Optimism After Recent Share Price Pullback?
summary: This article examines whether CME Group, trading at around US$287.65, reflects fair value or a potential mispricing opportunity by exploring what the current share price may be signaling. The stock has declined in the short term, with a 2.6% drop over 7 days and a 6.4% drop over 30 days, while still showing a 6.7% return year to date and a 13.8% return over 1 year. Recent attention on CME Group has focused on its role at the center of global derivatives trading, and on how shifts in trading...
